text:
Damn, the Yokai lore threads Oda is pulling for Mars are *gooood*.
In Japanese myth, the Itsumade is a demonic bird that appears in the night sky over lands plagued by famine, disease, and mass suffering. It hides in the fog and endlessly cries out, "Itsumade... Itsumade..." which basically translates to: "Until when?" or "When will it end?" Hence the name... That wasn't just some random freak weather that rotted Esperia's instruments and wrecked the country. That was Mars using a massive AOE.
It makes perfect sense mechanically, too. Mars’s official title is the "Warrior God of the Environment." Seeing him in that final panel basically confirms he was the one behind it. Tying that in with Shuri being Gunko, and the Celestial Dragon's not elevating random peasants... well, it's grim. Damn you Oda!
label: high_effort

text:
My hatred for the fucking World Government will never die. And those Marines. Fuck them too. They fight for slavers. Enough said.
I am glad that I was right about Candelle having nothing to do with the God's Knights. At least, I hope so.
I am sure she was targeted for being the strongest fighter in Esperia. It's clear the mist/fog was directed at weakening Esperia for an invasion.
Which is interesting, because it implies that the real reason for the invasion was not because Esperia wouldn't pay a tithe of slaves, but to create a situation where Reuven was put in a position to defy the WG. They created a casus belli.
So, little Brook was a pervert from the start. I won't say anything more about that, because I find that trope tiresome.
It is wonderful, though, how deeply affectionate the relationship between Shuri and Brook was, making this whole incident even more tragic and heartbreaking.
Speaking of which, clearly both Reuven and Shuri were Domi Reversi'd. But then why did Reversi'd Shuri kill Reversi'd Reuven? Was Reuven somehow breaking out of the control?
Not sure if the big, smoking dude is a Marine Admiral or a God's Knight. If the invasion wasn't about the refusal to pay the tithe, then was it about acquiring Shuri the whole time? Why?
And that's clearly one of the Gorosei standing behind the dead Reuven, right? What merited such immense power to be brought to topple Esperia?
I guess we'll find out. Hopefully.
Poor Brook. Goddamn, has he suffered. I hope he finally gets a win, and manages to free Shuri from Imu's control for good.
Edit: Multiple comments have pointed out the big clues Oda left that implies that a Manmayer washed up on Esperia, raped Candelle and Reuven married Candelle to protect her honour (what a guy). That's likely why the WG invaded Esperia. They wanted Shuri. Everything else was a ruse. I now feel even more strongly that Candelle was a D, and that's also why she was targeted and murdered by the killer fog.

text:
I think it's just the shock of it. It seems like a huge buff from a relatively short amount of training, and it's hard to reconcile that as a reader. But the visual information is there.
As a bit of meta-analysis, for those who simply can't believe that Luffy speed blitzed two Yonkou, I think it's worth considering the fact that - even if it was relatively short within the narrative - we actually had a training arc for Luffy prior to this showdown. That's pretty much unprecedented in One Piece. The closest thing we've really had, I think, was Luffy advancing his CoO during his fight with Katakuri, and that - too - resulted in a huge amount of growth for Luffy.
In the past, when faced with an opponent Luffy couldn't beat the first time around, he would ultimately gain victory through persistence. Opponents who showed themselves to be broadly superior fighters, like Crocodile or Lucci, were surpassed not through deliberate training, but by punching them more and harder. Wano is the first time we've been given a dedicated length of chapters to genuinely explore Luffy's concerted effort to grow stronger, and so of course the weight and effect of that training was always sure to be hugely significant, far beyond what growth we've seen from him in the past where no such time was dedicated.
All that said, this doesn't necessarily mean that Luffy is significantly faster than either Kaido or Big Mom. They clearly weren't expecting him to move like that, so their guards were probably down. Now they know what he's capable of, and once they get serious, Luffy and the gang are still definitely gonna have a rough time.
label: high_effort

Just some sources to corroborate what you're saying:
Chapter 585 page 12: Luffy says "I want to be..." and Sabo + Ace both laugh. At the time, I assumed he said he wanted to be pirate king.
Chapter 966 page 10: Roger says "That's right!! And then I'll be..." and Oden/Whitebeard respond in the same way as Ace/Sabo. There is clearly meant to be a parallel there.
Chapter 968 page 2: Roger says "King of the pirates has a nice ring to it" which when this chapter came out, many took to mean that this wasn't the goal he stated in 966, it's just a name someone else came up with that he liked.
Chapter 506 page 17: Shanks says: "there's a kid saying the exact same thing as captain Roger! The very same words the captain said!"

text:
Reviewed all his goofy move set and yes, this does amplifies Luffy’s goofy attacks.
Some people have been arguing that this seems to be a retcon of the fruit. I agree to the extent that it does appear to “retcon” the name of the fruit since our hint (correct me pls) is only around 1000+ BUT, outside of that, it stays consistent with Luffy! It is such a ridiculous power-up that only Luffy can harness it due to his creativity (e.g., his corkscrew attacks, ballooning, UFO kick, fire hydrant attack on Croco-boy, etc.)
Also, I don’t think that this is a straight “chosen one” trope because the fruit can be juxtaposed to Zoro’s Enma — they have wills of their own yet, ultimately, it depends upon the user to bring out its potential. Zoro was able to tame Enma, Luffy was able to do the same with Nika. It is still Luffy who’s in charge of wielding the fruit’s will, and not the other way around.

text:
It’s a retcon in that we didn’t get any inkling that Luffy’s fruit was different than a paramecia until what, The Who’s Who fight?
And every zoan that we’ve seen has followed the rules of a “human” form, full animal form, and hybrid. Or at the very least hasn’t broken it. Chopper is a potential issue but he has drugs to explain that away.
So what form has Luffy been in for the entire series? Is it hybrid? Are his gears hybrid? Or is this the one zoan we know of that doesn’t follow that paradigm?
I am extremely interested to see what comes of this, but I wish there was a way to see this coming before it happened.
